model_options.title: Device options
model_options.description: Select which SunSpec models (data registers) you want sensors for. The peak AC power is optional and used to discard unrealistic values that some inverters report at dawn or dusk (e.g. spikes in the MW or TWh range). Leave it empty to disable filtering.

data.capture_raw_registers: Capture raw modbus bytes for diagnostics
data.max_ac_power_kw: Peak AC power of the inverter (kW, optional)

transport_error.title: Cannot reach SunSpec inverter
transport_error.description:
The integration cannot connect to the SunSpec inverter at {host}:{port} (unit ID {unit_id}). Three consecutive update cycles have failed.

Last error: {error}

Things to check:
- The inverter is powered on
- The inverter is reachable on the network (try ping {host})
- No firewall is blocking TCP port {port}
- No other Modbus client is hogging the inverter's single TCP slot

protocol_error.title: Device does not speak SunSpec
protocol_error.description:
The integration reached the device at {host}:{port} (unit ID {unit_id}) but it does not respond as a SunSpec device.

Last error: {error}

Things to check:
- The device actually supports SunSpec (some Modbus devices use a vendor-specific register map)
- The unit ID is correct (some inverters use unit ID 1, others use 0 or 247)
- The device firmware is up to date - older firmware sometimes has broken SunSpec implementations

device_error.title: Inverter reports a Modbus exception
device_error.description:
The SunSpec inverter at {host}:{port} (unit ID {unit_id}) responded with a Modbus exception. Three consecutive update cycles have failed.

Last error: {error}

Things to check:
- The inverter is in a normal operating state (not in fault mode)
- The selected enabled models match what the inverter actually supports
- Download diagnostics from this integration and attach them to a bug report

cjne_conflict.title: cjne/ha-sunspec is still active
cjne_conflict.description:
SunSpec 2 cannot start up for the inverter at {host}:{port} (unit ID {unit_id}) because the cjne/ha-sunspec integration is still actively running for the same device.

KACO Powador and most other SunSpec inverters only allow ONE Modbus TCP connection at a time. cjne is currently holding that connection, so SunSpec 2 cannot poll the device. Running both integrations against the same inverter would also produce duplicate sensors and unstable values.

To migrate from cjne to SunSpec 2:

1. Open HACS and uninstall the cjne/ha-sunspec integration
2. Restart Home Assistant
3. SunSpec 2 will retry automatically and pick up the existing entities

Your entity IDs and Recorder history will be preserved by the SunSpec 2 migration helper.
